objective: Harden production egress evidence validation so placeholders, templates, and documentation/example CIDRs cannot satisfy release evidence gates.
rollbackNotes: Preserve unrelated dirty changes; revert only this checkpoint scope if explicitly approved.
nextAction: Continue with next highest-priority READY item: kafka-dlq-sharding.
implementationSummary: Production egress validator now rejects template/example filenames and placeholder text in reviewer/rule fields.
Validator requires schemaVersion 1, parseable non-future reviewedAt, canonical CIDR notation, and rejects broad, reserved, documentation, non-canonical, or unsupported FQDN evidence.
Regression self-test now covers valid public CIDR and reviewed private endpoint evidence plus template, broad, documentation, placeholder, future-date, non-canonical, and FQDN failure cases.
Release and validation docs now state that the checked-in template is a negative test, not strict promotion evidence.
residualRisks: This validator rejects obvious fake evidence but does not create or verify real target-environment egress evidence.
Strict release validation still needs a follow-up to prove reviewed external egress policy generation/inclusion in the production render.
Production release remains blocked until all external evidence requirements are collected and strict release gates pass.
